pages.title: Best 0% APR Credit Cards
pages.description: The best 0% intro APR credit cards for purchases and balance transfers, all with no annual fee.
pages.seo_description: Compare the best 0% intro APR credit cards of 2026. Up to 24 months of 0% APR on purchases and balance transfers.
pages.intro: A 0% intro APR card lets you make purchases or transfer balances without paying interest for an extended period — typically 12 to 24 months. These no-annual-fee cards are ideal for financing a large purchase over time or consolidating existing credit card debt.

cards.highlight: 24 months of 0% APR on both purchases and balance transfers — the longest intro period available. With no annual fee, this card provides nearly two full years of interest-free financing for major purchases or debt consolidation.
21 months of 0% APR on both purchases and balance transfers with no annual fee. Offers a competitive dual intro period just 3 months shorter than the market leader.

18 months of 0% APR on both purchases and balance transfers with no annual fee and a 14.49% regular APR floor — the lowest ongoing rate on this list. Solid interest-free window combined with the most competitive post-intro APR available.
21 months of 0% APR on balance transfers with 12 months on purchases, plus no late fees ever. Ideal for consolidating existing credit card debt without the risk of late payment penalties.

21 months of 0% APR on balance transfers and 12 months on purchases, with no annual fee. Offers strong balance transfer terms at a slightly lower regular APR range (16.49%-27.24%) compared to Citi Simplicity.
21 months of 0% APR on balance transfers only, with no intro period on new purchases. Regular APR range of 22.49%-31.24% is higher than most competitors, limiting its appeal.

pages.title: Best Airline Credit Cards
pages.description: The top co-branded airline credit cards ranked by signup bonuses, earning rates, and travel perks.
pages.seo_description: Compare the best airline credit cards of 2026. Delta, United, American, Southwest, JetBlue, and more — ranked by value.
pages.intro: Airline credit cards earn miles or points in a specific loyalty program and come with perks like free checked bags, priority boarding, and lounge access. We've ranked these by overall value—considering signup bonuses, spending requirements, annual fees, earning rates, and travel benefits—to help you find the best fit whether you're a frequent flyer or casual traveler.

cards.highlight: 30,000-mile signup bonus after $1,000 spend in 3 months with no annual fee. Earns 2x miles on United, gas, and transit, plus 1x on everything else.
60,000-point signup bonus after $1,000 spend in 3 months for $99/year. Earns 6x points on JetBlue flights, 2x on dining and groceries, plus free checked bag and 50% inflight savings.
10,000 points with no annual fee after $1,000 spend in 3 months. Earns 3x on JetBlue, 2x on dining and groceries, 1x on everything else.
15,000 AAdvantage miles with no annual fee after $1,000 spend in 3 months. Earns 2x miles on American Airlines and groceries, 1x on everything else.
50,000 AAdvantage miles after $2,500 spend in 3 months for $99/year. Earns 2x miles on airlines, dining, and gas, plus free checked bag and preferred boarding.
10,000 SkyMiles with no annual fee after $1,000 spend in 6 months. Earns 2x miles on Delta and dining, 1x on everything else.
50,000 Rapid Rewards points after $1,000 spend in 3 months for $99/year. Earns 2x points on Southwest, gas, and groceries, with anniversary points toward the Companion Pass.
80,000 SkyMiles after $2,000 spend in 6 months for $150/year. Earns 2x miles on Delta, dining, and groceries, plus free first checked bag and companion baggage fees covered.
50,000 miles after $2,000 spend in 3 months for $99/year. Earns 3x on Hawaiian Airlines, 2x on dining, gas, and groceries, plus 0% APR on balance transfers for 15 months.
70,000-mile signup bonus after $3,000 spend in 3 months for $150/year. Earns 3x miles on United flights, 2x on dining and hotels, plus free checked bag and two annual United Club passes.
55,000 Rapid Rewards points after $1,500 spend in 3 months for $149/year. Earns 3x on Southwest flights, 2x on groceries and dining, plus anniversary points and 4 upgraded boardings annually.
75,000 Avios after $5,000 spend in 3 months for $95/year. Earns 3x on British Airways, Aer Lingus, and Iberia flights, 2x on hotels, 1x elsewhere.

60,000 Rapid Rewards points, $75 annual travel credit, 4 upgraded boardings, and 7,500 anniversary points after $2,000 spend in 3 months for $229/year. Earns 4x on Southwest flights, 2x on gas and dining.
90,000 miles and up to $250 annual travel credits after $4,000 spend in 3 months for $350/year. Earns 4x on United flights, 5x through hotel portals, 2x on travel, dining, and streaming.
90,000 AAdvantage miles after $5,000 spend in 4 months for $350/year. Earns 6x on hotel and car bookings through Citi portals, 3x on American Airlines, 2x on dining and transit.
90,000 SkyMiles and companion certificate after $3,000 spend in 6 months for $350/year. Earns 3x on Delta flights and hotels, 2x on dining and groceries, 1x elsewhere.
70,000 AAdvantage miles and Admirals Club lounge access after $7,000 spend in 3 months for $595/year. Earns 4x on American Airlines, 10x on Citi portal hotel and car bookings.
100,000 SkyMiles and companion certificate after $5,000 spend in 6 months for $650/year, plus Delta Sky Club membership. Earns 3x on Delta flights and hotels, 1x elsewhere.
100,000 miles and United Club Infinite lounge access after $5,000 spend in 3 months for $695/year. Earns 5x on United flights, 2x on dining and travel, 1.5x on everything else.

pages.title: Best Cash Back Credit Cards
pages.description: The top cash back credit cards ranked by earning rates, bonus categories, and signup offers — all with no annual fee unless noted.
pages.seo_title: Best Cash Back Credit Cards of 2026
pages.seo_description: Compare the best cash back credit cards of 2026. Flat-rate 2% cards, 5% rotating categories, and the best signup bonuses.
pages.intro: Cash back cards offer the simplest way to earn rewards — direct cash with no points to transfer or portals to navigate. We've ranked the best options based on signup bonus value and ease of earning it, ongoing cash back rates, and whether any annual fee is justified by the rewards you'll actually earn.

cards.highlight: Earn $200 cash back after just $500 in spending, then enjoy 5% back on rotating quarterly categories and Chase Travel, 3% on dining and drugstores, and 1% on everything else—all with no annual fee and a 15-month intro APR on purchases.

Earn 5% cash back on rotating quarterly categories and 1% on everything else, with Discover matching all cash back earned in your first year to effectively double your rewards. No annual fee, no signup minimum, and 15-month intro APR on purchases.

Earn $200 cash back after $500 in spending, then get 1.5% back on all purchases plus 3% on dining and drugstores and 5% on Chase Travel—with no annual fee and 15-month intro APR on purchases and balance transfers.
Get 2% cash back on every purchase with no annual fee, no spending caps, and no categories to track. Earn a $200 bonus after $500 in spending.

Earn 2% cash back on all purchases through a unique 1%-on-purchase, 1%-on-payment structure, plus 5% on Chase Travel. Get a $200 bonus after $1,500 in 6 months with no annual fee and 18-month intro APR on balance transfers.
Earn 3% cash back at U.S. supermarkets (up to $6,000 yearly), 3% on gas, 3% on online shopping, and 1% elsewhere. Get a $200 bonus after $2,000 in 6 months with no annual fee.

Earn 5% cash back on your highest spending category each month (from dining, gas, groceries, transit, streaming, and more, capped at $500/month) plus 1% elsewhere. Get a $200 bonus after $1,500 in 6 months with no annual fee.
Earn 5% cash back at Amazon and Whole Foods (for Prime members), 5% on Chase Travel, 2% on dining, gas, and transit, and 1% elsewhere. Get a $150 bonus with no spending requirement.
Earn 3% cash back on dining, travel, gas, transit, and streaming, plus 1% elsewhere. Get a $200 equivalent bonus (20,000 points) after $1,000 in 3 months with no annual fee.
Earn 4% cash back on fitness, wellness, sports, live events, pet supplies, transit, and EV charging, plus 1% elsewhere. Get a $100 bonus after $500 in spending with no annual fee.
Earn 3% cash back on dining, groceries, entertainment, and streaming, plus 1% elsewhere. Get a $200 bonus after $500 in spending, but includes a $39 annual fee.
Earn 2% cash back on all business purchases with no annual fee and no spending caps. Get a $500 bonus after $5,000 in 3 months—the highest flat bonus available.
1.5% cash back on everything with no annual fee, no rotating categories, and no spending caps. Earn a $200 bonus after $500 in spending.

pages.title: Best Credit Cards for Dining and Groceries
pages.description: The top credit cards for earning rewards on restaurants and grocery store purchases, from premium to no-annual-fee options.
pages.seo_title: Best Credit Cards for Dining and Groceries of 2026
pages.seo_description: Compare the best dining and grocery credit cards of 2026. Earn 3-4x points or up to 6% cash back on food spending.
pages.intro: Dining and groceries represent major spending categories for most households. The right card can earn substantial rewards on these purchases, whether you prioritize maximum earning rates, signup bonuses, or cards without annual fees.

cards.highlight: Earns 4x Membership Rewards points on both dining and groceries—the highest ongoing earn rate available. A $325 annual fee is offset by $240 in combined dining and Uber credits, plus a 100,000-point signup bonus on $8,000 spend.

Earns 6% cash back at U.S. supermarkets up to $6,000/year, then 1%—the highest grocery earn rate available—plus 6% on streaming and 3% on transit and gas. A $95 annual fee is offset by a $300 cash signup bonus on $3,000 spend and 12-month 0% APR on purchases.

Earns 4x points on dining up to $2,000/quarter, then 1x—the highest no-fee dining earn rate available—plus 2x on groceries, gas, and streaming with no annual fee. Includes a 20,000-point signup bonus on just $1,000 spend.

Earns 5% cash back on your top spending category each billing cycle—including dining or groceries (up to $500/month)—with no annual fee. Includes a $200 cash bonus on $1,500 spend and 15-month 0% APR on purchases and balance transfers.

Earns 3% cash back at U.S. supermarkets up to $6,000/year, then 1%, with no annual fee, plus 3% on gas and online shopping. Includes a $200 cash bonus on $2,000 spend and 15-month 0% APR on purchases.

Earns 3% cash back on dining and groceries (plus entertainment and streaming) with a $39 annual fee. Includes a $200 cash bonus on just $500 spend and 12-month 0% APR on purchases and balance transfers.
Earns 3x points on both dining and groceries, plus 3x on airlines, hotels, and gas for a $95 annual fee. Includes a 60,000-point signup bonus on $4,000 spend.

Earns 3x points on dining, but only 3x on online grocery purchases (not in-store), for a $95 annual fee. Includes a 75,000-point signup bonus on $5,000 spend with point transfers to travel partners like Hyatt and United.
Earns 3x points on dining with no annual fee, plus 3x on travel, gas, transit, and streaming, but only 1x on groceries. Includes a 20,000-point bonus on $1,000 spend.
Earns 3% cash back on dining with no annual fee, plus 1.5% on everything else. Includes a $200 cash bonus on $500 spend and 15-month 0% APR on purchases and balance transfers.
Earns 3% cash back on dining with no annual fee, plus rotating 5% categories that sometimes include grocery stores. Includes a $200 cash bonus on $500 spend and 15-month 0% APR on purchases.

Earns 2x miles on dining and U.S. supermarkets for a $150 annual fee, with an 80,000-mile signup bonus on $2,000 spend. This card is better suited for frequent Delta flyers than for optimizing general dining-and-grocery rewards.
Earns 3x points on dining but only 1x on groceries for a $795 annual fee plus premium travel benefits. Includes a 150,000-point signup bonus on $6,000 spend.

pages.title: Best Secured Credit Cards
pages.description: The best secured credit cards for building or rebuilding credit, all with no annual fee.
pages.seo_description: Compare the best secured credit cards of 2026 for building credit. All have $0 annual fees and report to all three bureaus.
pages.intro: Secured credit cards require a refundable security deposit that becomes your credit limit, making them ideal for building or rebuilding credit. The best options report to all three major credit bureaus, charge no annual fee, and offer rewards to help you maximize spending while rebuilding credit.

cards.highlight: Earns 2% cash back at gas stations and restaurants (up to $1,000/quarter), then 1% on everything else. Discover matches all cash back earned in your first year, effectively doubling your rewards. No annual fee.

Earns 3% cash back in one category you select, 2% at grocery stores and wholesale clubs, and 1% on everything else. No annual fee with flexible quarterly category changes to match your spending patterns.
Earns a flat 1.5% cash back on all purchases with no rotating categories to track. Includes 5% cash back on hotels and car rentals through Capital One Travel. No annual fee.
Designed for accessible credit building with deposits as low as $49 depending on creditworthiness. No annual fee, no rewards, and straightforward terms for establishing credit history.
A straightforward secured Mastercard with no annual fee and no rewards. Reports to all three major credit bureaus and offers a documented upgrade path to unsecured Citi products.
A basic secured Visa with no annual fee and no rewards. U.S. Bank automatically reviews accounts for potential upgrade to an unsecured card.

pages.title: Best Credit Card Signup Bonuses
pages.description: The most valuable credit card signup bonuses available right now, ranked by bonus value and overall appeal.
pages.seo_title: Best Credit Card Signup Bonuses of 2026
pages.seo_description: Compare the best credit card signup bonuses of 2026. Ranked by value — from 175k points to $200 cash back.
pages.intro: Signup bonuses are the fastest way to earn valuable rewards from a new credit card. We've ranked the best offers available right now based on bonus value, spending requirements, annual fee efficiency, and ongoing rewards—helping you find the best option whether you prioritize points, miles, or cash back.

cards.highlight: 175,000 Membership Rewards points after $12,000 in 6 months — worth roughly $3,500 when transferred to airline and hotel partners. This is the highest-value signup bonus available by a significant margin, and 5x earning on airfare and hotels makes it excellent for premium travel spending.

150,000 Ultimate Rewards points after $6,000 in 3 months — worth up to $2,250 through Chase Travel. The $300 annual travel credit significantly offsets the $795 fee, and 8x points on Chase Travel portal bookings provide excellent ongoing value.

75,000 Ultimate Rewards points after $5,000 in 3 months — worth at least $1,125 through Chase Travel. The $95 annual fee and transfer-friendly points make this an excellent value for casual to moderate travelers seeking premium rewards without high annual costs.

75,000 miles after $4,000 in 3 months, plus $300 annual travel credit and 10,000 anniversary miles. The effective annual cost drops to just $95 after travel credits, and 10x earning on hotel and car bookings through the portal delivers strong ongoing value.
100,000 Membership Rewards points after $8,000 in 6 months. Strong 4x earning on dining and groceries pairs with the $325 annual fee for compelling everyday rewards beyond the signup bonus.

100,000 Ultimate Rewards points after $8,000 in 3 months — worth up to $1,500 through Chase Travel. At just $95 annual fee, this delivers one of the best business card values with flexible transfer-friendly rewards.

150,000 IHG points after $3,000 in 3 months — worth roughly $750 in hotel stays. The $99 annual fee and easy spend requirement make this one of the most attainable hotel bonuses, with 26x earning at IHG properties.

60,000 World of Hyatt points after $5,000 in 3 months — worth $1,200+ in hotel stays at roughly 2 cents per point. Hyatt points consistently offer the highest redemption value among hotel loyalty programs.
140,000 IHG points after $4,000 in 3 months — worth roughly $700 in hotel stays. The $99 annual fee and 26x earning at IHG properties match the personal version for business travelers.
90,000 SkyMiles after $3,000 in 6 months, plus a companion certificate and 3x earning on airlines and hotels. The $350 annual fee makes this a mid-tier option for Delta-focused travelers.
90,000 United miles after $4,000 in 3 months. Includes two free checked bags annually and 25% back on United award flights, with strong benefits for United-focused travelers.
80,000 SkyMiles after just $2,000 in 6 months at only $150 annual fee. This is the most affordable entry point into the Delta ecosystem for casual and emerging flyers.
100,000 Delta SkyMiles after $5,000 in 6 months, plus Delta Sky Club access and a companion certificate. The $650 annual fee is justified for frequent Delta flyers with strong perks.
$500 cash back after $5,000 in 3 months with zero annual fee. Simple 2% flat cash back on all business purchases with no categories to track.
$200 cash back after just $500 in spending — one of the easiest bonuses to earn. Zero annual fee with 5% rotating categories, 5% on Chase Travel portal, and 3% on dining and drugstores.
$200 cash back after $500 in 3 months with zero annual fee. Straightforward 2% cash back on all purchases plus 12-month 0% APR on purchases and balance transfers.
$200 cash back after $500 in 3 months. Offers 3% on dining, entertainment, streaming, and groceries with a $39 annual fee.

pages.title: Best Travel Credit Cards
pages.description: The top travel credit cards ranked by rewards, travel perks, and overall value — from premium to no-annual-fee options.
pages.seo_description: Compare the best travel credit cards of 2026. From Chase Sapphire Reserve to no-fee options — ranked by rewards and perks.
pages.intro: The best travel cards earn accelerated rewards on flights, hotels, and dining while offering premium perks like lounge access and travel credits. We've ranked these by overall value for travelers—considering signup bonuses, ongoing rewards, annual fees, and transfer partner flexibility.

cards.highlight: Earns 5x points on Chase Travel portal, 3x on dining and streaming, and 2x on travel purchases. The 75,000-point signup bonus after $5,000 spend delivers strong upfront value, and the $95 annual fee is easily offset by everyday rewards.

Earns 3x points on dining, travel, gas, transit, and streaming with zero annual fee. The 20,000-point signup bonus after just $1,000 spend and 12-month 0% APR intro period make this the most accessible option for budget-conscious travelers.

Earns 10x points on hotels and cars through Citi Travel, 3x on airlines, hotels, dining, groceries, and gas, and 1x elsewhere. The 60,000-point signup bonus after $4,000 spend provides strong portal value at just $95 annually.

Earns 8x on Chase Travel portal, 4x on direct travel purchases, and 3x on dining, with a $300 annual travel credit and Priority Pass lounge access. The 150,000-point signup bonus after $6,000 spend justifies the $795 annual fee for frequent international travelers.

Earns 10x points on hotels and cars and 5x on flights through Capital One's portal, plus 2x on everything else. The $300 annual travel credit and 10,000 annual bonus miles effectively reduce the $395 fee, making it compelling for frequent luxury hotel and flight bookers.

Earns 5x points on hotels and cars through the portal and 2x on all other purchases with no category limits. The 75,000-mile signup bonus after $4,000 spend delivers strong value at just $95 annually.

Earns 12x on hotels and cars through Citi Travel, 6x on flights booked through the portal, and 3x on dining. The 75,000-point signup bonus after $6,000 spend offers strong portal value, though the $595 annual fee requires significant travel spending to break even.

Earns 4x on dining and groceries, 3x on flights booked directly, and 5x on hotels through the portal. Monthly dining and Uber credits reduce the $325 annual fee, with a 100,000-point signup bonus after $8,000 spend over 6 months.

Earns 3x on dining, 2x on travel and all other purchases, and uniquely earns points on rent payments. The 50,000-point signup bonus after $4,000 spend transfers to partners like Hyatt and United at $495 annually.
Earns 3x on airlines, dining, and foreign transactions, plus 1x elsewhere. The 100,000-point signup bonus after $4,000 spend emphasizes international travel benefits at $395 annually.
Earns 3x on airlines, 2x on gas, streaming, and transit, plus 1x on everything else. The 50,000-point signup bonus after just $2,000 spend at $95 annually makes this an accessible mid-tier option.
Earns 4x on dining, 2x on groceries, gas, and streaming, and 1x elsewhere with no annual fee. The 20,000-point signup bonus after $1,000 spend includes 15-month intro 0% APR on purchases.

Earns a flat 1.5x points on all purchases with no annual fee and no foreign transaction fees. The 25,000-point signup bonus after $1,000 spend and 15-month intro 0% APR suit occasional travelers seeking simplicity.

Earns 4x points at Hyatt properties and 2x on dining, with automatic Discoverist elite status. The 60,000-point signup bonus after $5,000 spend and $199 annual fee are best for dedicated Hyatt members.
